info.name: Matthew
info.desc: The Holy Gospel According to Matthew

1:
This is the genealogy
 of Jesus the Messiah
 the son of David, the son of Abraham:
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
1:1 Or Jesus Christ. Messiah (Hebrew) and Christ (Greek) both mean Anointed One; also in verse 18.; 1:1 Or is an account of the origin
2:
Abraham was the father of Isaac,
Isaac the father of Jacob,
Jacob the father of Judah and his brothers,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
3:
Judah the father of Perez and Zerah, whose mother was Tamar,
Perez the father of Hezron,
Hezron the father of Ram,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
4:
Ram the father of Amminadab,
Amminadab the father of Nahshon,
Nahshon the father of Salmon,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
5:
Salmon the father of Boaz, whose mother was Rahab,
Boaz the father of Obed, whose mother was Ruth,
Obed the father of Jesse,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
6:
and Jesse the father of King David.
David was the father of Solomon, whose mother had been Uriah’s wife,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
7:
Solomon the father of Rehoboam,
Rehoboam the father of Abijah,
Abijah the father of Asa,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
8:
Asa the father of Jehoshaphat,
Jehoshaphat the father of Jehoram,
Jehoram the father of Uzziah,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
9:
Uzziah the father of Jotham,
Jotham the father of Ahaz,
Ahaz the father of Hezekiah,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
10:
Hezekiah the father of Manasseh,
Manasseh the father of Amon,
Amon the father of Josiah,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
11:
and Josiah the father of Jeconiah
 and his brothers at the time of the exile to Babylon.
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
1:11 That is, Jehoiachin; also in verse 12
12:
After the exile to Babylon:
Jeconiah was the father of Shealtiel,
Shealtiel the father of Zerubbabel,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
13:
Zerubbabel the father of Abihud,
Abihud the father of Eliakim,
Eliakim the father of Azor,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
14:
Azor the father of Zadok,
Zadok the father of Akim,
Akim the father of Elihud,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
15:
Elihud the father of Eleazar,
Eleazar the father of Matthan,
Matthan the father of Jacob,
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
16: and Jacob the father of Joseph, the husband of Mary, and Mary was the mother of Jesus who is called the Messiah.
The Genealogy of Jesus the Messiah
17: Thus there were fourteen generations in all from Abraham to David, fourteen from David to the exile to Babylon, and fourteen from the exile to the Messiah.
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son
18:
This is how the birth of Jesus the Messiah came about
: His mother Mary was pledged to be married to Joseph, but before they came together, she was found to be pregnant through the Holy Spirit.
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son
1:18 Or The origin of Jesus the Messiah was like this
19:
Because Joseph her husband was faithful to the law, and yet
 did not want to expose her to public disgrace, he had in mind to divorce her quietly.
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son
1:19 Or was a righteous man and
20: But after he had considered this, an angel of the Lord appeared to him in a dream and said, “Joseph son of David, do not be afraid to take Mary home as your wife, because what is conceived in her is from the Holy Spirit.
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son
21:
She will give birth to a son, and you are to give him the name Jesus,
 because he will save his people from their sins.”
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son
1:21 Jesus is the Greek form of Joshua, which means the Lord saves.
22: All this took place to fulfill what the Lord had said through the prophet:
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son
23:
“The virgin will conceive and give birth to a son, and they will call him Immanuel”
 (which means “God with us”).
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son
1:23 Isaiah 7:14
24: When Joseph woke up, he did what the angel of the Lord had commanded him and took Mary home as his wife.
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son
25: But he did not consummate their marriage until she gave birth to a son. And he gave him the name Jesus.
Joseph Accepts Jesus as His Son

1:
After Jesus was born in Bethlehem in Judea, during the time of King Herod, Magi
 from the east came to Jerusalem
The Magi Visit the Messiah
2:1 Traditionally wise men
2: and asked, “Where is the one who has been born king of the Jews? We saw his star when it rose and have come to worship him.”
The Magi Visit the Messiah
3: When King Herod heard this he was disturbed, and all Jerusalem with him.
The Magi Visit the Messiah
4: When he had called together all the people’s chief priests and teachers of the law, he asked them where the Messiah was to be born.
The Magi Visit the Messiah
5: “In Bethlehem in Judea,” they replied, “for this is what the prophet has written:
The Magi Visit the Messiah
6:
“ ‘But you, Bethlehem, in the land of Judah,
are by no means least among the rulers of Judah;
for out of you will come a ruler
who will shepherd my people Israel.’
”
The Magi Visit the Messiah
7: Then Herod called the Magi secretly and found out from them the exact time the star had appeared.
The Magi Visit the Messiah
8: He sent them to Bethlehem and said, “Go and search carefully for the child. As soon as you find him, report to me, so that I too may go and worship him.”
The Magi Visit the Messiah
9: After they had heard the king, they went on their way, and the star they had seen when it rose went ahead of them until it stopped over the place where the child was.
The Magi Visit the Messiah
10: When they saw the star, they were overjoyed.
The Magi Visit the Messiah
11: On coming to the house, they saw the child with his mother Mary, and they bowed down and worshiped him. Then they opened their treasures and presented him with gifts of gold, frankincense and myrrh.
The Magi Visit the Messiah
12: And having been warned in a dream not to go back to Herod, they returned to their country by another route.
The Escape to Egypt
13: When they had gone, an angel of the Lord appeared to Joseph in a dream. “Get up,” he said, “take the child and his mother and escape to Egypt. Stay there until I tell you, for Herod is going to search for the child to kill him.”
The Escape to Egypt
14: So he got up, took the child and his mother during the night and left for Egypt,
The Escape to Egypt
15: where he stayed until the death of Herod. And so was fulfilled what the Lord had said through the prophet: “Out of Egypt I called my son.”
The Escape to Egypt
16: When Herod realized that he had been outwitted by the Magi, he was furious, and he gave orders to kill all the boys in Bethlehem and its vicinity who were two years old and under, in accordance with the time he had learned from the Magi.
The Escape to Egypt
17: Then what was said through the prophet Jeremiah was fulfilled:
The Escape to Egypt
18:
“A voice is heard in Ramah,
weeping and great mourning,
Rachel weeping for her children
and refusing to be comforted,
because they are no more.”
The Return to Nazareth
19: After Herod died, an angel of the Lord appeared in a dream to Joseph in Egypt
The Return to Nazareth
20: and said, “Get up, take the child and his mother and go to the land of Israel, for those who were trying to take the child’s life are dead.”
The Return to Nazareth
21: So he got up, took the child and his mother and went to the land of Israel.
The Return to Nazareth
22: But when he heard that Archelaus was reigning in Judea in place of his father Herod, he was afraid to go there. Having been warned in a dream, he withdrew to the district of Galilee,
The Return to Nazareth
23: and he went and lived in a town called Nazareth. So was fulfilled what was said through the prophets, that he would be called a Nazarene.
The Return to Nazareth

1: In those days John the Baptist came, preaching in the wilderness of Judea
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
2: and saying, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
3:
This is he who was spoken of through the prophet Isaiah:
“A voice of one calling in the wilderness,
‘Prepare the way for the Lord,
make straight paths for him.’ ”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
; 3:3 Isaiah 40:3
4: John’s clothes were made of camel’s hair, and he had a leather belt around his waist. His food was locusts and wild honey.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
5: People went out to him from Jerusalem and all Judea and the whole region of the Jordan.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
6: Confessing their sins, they were baptized by him in the Jordan River.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
7: But when he saw many of the Pharisees and Sadducees coming to where he was baptizing, he said to them: “You brood of vipers! Who warned you to flee from the coming wrath?
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
8: Produce fruit in keeping with repentance.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
9: And do not think you can say to yourselves, ‘We have Abraham as our father.’ I tell you that out of these stones God can raise up children for Abraham.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
10: The ax is already at the root of the trees, and every tree that does not produce good fruit will be cut down and thrown into the fire.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
11:
“I baptize you with
 water for repentance. But after me comes one who is more powerful than I, whose sandals I am not worthy to carry. He will baptize you with
 the Holy Spirit and fire.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
3:11 Or in
12: His winnowing fork is in his hand, and he will clear his threshing floor, gathering his wheat into the barn and burning up the chaff with unquenchable fire.”
The Baptism of Jesus
13: Then Jesus came from Galilee to the Jordan to be baptized by John.
The Baptism of Jesus
14: But John tried to deter him, saying, “I need to be baptized by you, and do you come to me?”
The Baptism of Jesus
15: Jesus replied, “Let it be so now; it is proper for us to do this to fulfill all righteousness.” Then John consented.
The Baptism of Jesus
16: As soon as Jesus was baptized, he went up out of the water. At that moment heaven was opened, and he saw the Spirit of God descending like a dove and alighting on him.
The Baptism of Jesus
17: And a voice from heaven said, “This is my Son, whom I love; with him I am well pleased.”
The Baptism of Jesus

1:
Then Jesus was led by the Spirit into the wilderness to be tempted
 by the devil.
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
4:1 The Greek for tempted can also mean tested.
2: After fasting forty days and forty nights, he was hungry.
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
3: The tempter came to him and said, “If you are the Son of God, tell these stones to become bread.”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
4: Jesus answered, “It is written: ‘Man shall not live on bread alone, but on every word that comes from the mouth of God.’ ”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
5: Then the devil took him to the holy city and had him stand on the highest point of the temple.
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
6:
“If you are the Son of God,” he said, “throw yourself down. For it is written:
“ ‘He will command his angels concerning you,
and they will lift you up in their hands,
so that you will not strike your foot against a stone.’
”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
7: Jesus answered him, “It is also written: ‘Do not put the Lord your God to the test.’ ”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
8: Again, the devil took him to a very high mountain and showed him all the kingdoms of the world and their splendor.
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
9: “All this I will give you,” he said, “if you will bow down and worship me.”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
10: Jesus said to him, “Away from me, Satan! For it is written: ‘Worship the Lord your God, and serve him only.’ ”
Jesus Is Tested in the Wilderness
11: Then the devil left him, and angels came and attended him.
Jesus Begins to Preach
12: When Jesus heard that John had been put in prison, he withdrew to Galilee.
Jesus Begins to Preach
13: Leaving Nazareth, he went and lived in Capernaum, which was by the lake in the area of Zebulun and Naphtali—
Jesus Begins to Preach
14: to fulfill what was said through the prophet Isaiah:
Jesus Begins to Preach
15:
“Land of Zebulun and land of Naphtali,
the Way of the Sea, beyond the Jordan,
Galilee of the Gentiles—
Jesus Begins to Preach
16:
the people living in darkness
have seen a great light;
on those living in the land of the shadow of death
a light has dawned.”
Jesus Begins to Preach
17: From that time on Jesus began to preach, “Repent, for the kingdom of heaven has come near.”
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
18: As Jesus was walking beside the Sea of Galilee, he saw two brothers, Simon called Peter and his brother Andrew. They were casting a net into the lake, for they were fishermen.
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
19: “Come, follow me,” Jesus said, “and I will send you out to fish for people.”
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
20: At once they left their nets and followed him.
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
21: Going on from there, he saw two other brothers, James son of Zebedee and his brother John. They were in a boat with their father Zebedee, preparing their nets. Jesus called them,
Jesus Calls His First Disciples
22: and immediately they left the boat and their father and followed him.
Jesus Heals the Sick
23: Jesus went throughout Galilee, teaching in their synagogues, proclaiming the good news of the kingdom, and healing every disease and sickness among the people.
Jesus Heals the Sick
24: News about him spread all over Syria, and people brought to him all who were ill with various diseases, those suffering severe pain, the demon-possessed, those having seizures, and the paralyzed; and he healed them.
Jesus Heals the Sick
25:
Large crowds from Galilee, the Decapolis,
 Jerusalem, Judea and the region across the Jordan followed him.
Jesus Heals the Sick
4:25 That is, the Ten Cities

1: Now when Jesus saw the crowds, he went up on a mountainside and sat down. His disciples came to him,
Introduction to the Sermon on the Mount
2:
and he began to teach them.
He said:
The Beatitudes
3: “Blessed are the poor in spirit, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
The Beatitudes
4: Blessed are those who mourn, for they will be comforted.
The Beatitudes
5: Blessed are the meek, for they will inherit the earth.
The Beatitudes
6: Blessed are those who hunger and thirst for righteousness, for they will be filled.
The Beatitudes
7: Blessed are the merciful, for they will be shown mercy.
The Beatitudes
8: Blessed are the pure in heart, for they will see God.
The Beatitudes
9: Blessed are the peacemakers, for they will be called children of God.
The Beatitudes
10: Blessed are those who are persecuted because of righteousness, for theirs is the kingdom of heaven.
The Beatitudes
11: “Blessed are you when people insult you, persecute you and falsely say all kinds of evil against you because of me.
The Beatitudes
12: Rejoice and be glad, because great is your reward in heaven, for in the same way they persecuted the prophets who were before you.
Salt and Light
13: “You are the salt of the earth. But if the salt loses its saltiness, how can it be made salty again? It is no longer good for anything, except to be thrown out and trampled underfoot.
Salt and Light
14: “You are the light of the world. A town built on a hill cannot be hidden.
Salt and Light
15: Neither do people light a lamp and put it under a bowl. Instead they put it on its stand, and it gives light to everyone in the house.
Salt and Light
16: In the same way, let your light shine before others, that they may see your good deeds and glorify your Father in heaven.
The Fulfillment of the Law
17: “Do not think that I have come to abolish the Law or the Prophets; I have not come to abolish them but to fulfill them.
The Fulfillment of the Law
18: For truly I tell you, until heaven and earth disappear, not the smallest letter, not the least stroke of a pen, will by any means disappear from the Law until everything is accomplished.
The Fulfillment of the Law
19: Therefore anyone who sets aside one of the least of these commands and teaches others accordingly will be called least in the kingdom of heaven, but whoever practices and teaches these commands will be called great in the kingdom of heaven.
The Fulfillment of the Law
20: For I tell you that unless your righteousness surpasses that of the Pharisees and the teachers of the law, you will certainly not enter the kingdom of heaven.
Murder
21: “You have heard that it was said to the people long ago, ‘You shall not murder, and anyone who murders will be subject to judgment.’
Murder
5:21              Exodus 20:13
22: But I tell you that anyone who is angry with a brother or sister will be subject to judgment. Again, anyone who says to a brother or sister, ‘ Raca ,’ is answerable to the court. And anyone who says, ‘You fool!’ will be in danger of the fire of hell.
Murder

; 5:22 An Aramaic term of contempt; 5:22 The Greek word for brother or sister (adelphos) refers here to a fellow disciple, whether man or woman; also in verse 23.; 5:22 Some manuscripts brother or sister without cause
23: “Therefore, if you are offering your gift at the altar and there remember that your brother or sister has something against you,
Murder
24: leave your gift there in front of the altar. First go and be reconciled to them; then come and offer your gift.
Murder
25: “Settle matters quickly with your adversary who is taking you to court. Do it while you are still together on the way, or your adversary may hand you over to the judge, and the judge may hand you over to the officer, and you may be thrown into prison.
Murder
26: Truly I tell you, you will not get out until you have paid the last penny.
Adultery
27: “You have heard that it was said, ‘You shall not commit adultery.’
Adultery
5:27              Exodus 20:14
28: But I tell you that anyone who looks at a woman lustfully has already committed adultery with her in his heart.
Adultery
29: If your right eye causes you to stumble, gouge it out and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to be thrown into hell.
Adultery
30: And if your right hand causes you to stumble, cut it off and throw it away. It is better for you to lose one part of your body than for your whole body to go into hell.
Divorce
31: “It has been said, ‘Anyone who divorces his wife must give her a certificate of divorce.’
Divorce
32: But I tell you that anyone who divorces his wife, except for sexual immorality, makes her the victim of adultery, and anyone who marries a divorced woman commits adultery.
Oaths
33: “Again, you have heard that it was said to the people long ago, ‘Do not break your oath, but fulfill to the Lord the vows you have made.’
Oaths
34: But I tell you, do not swear an oath at all: either by heaven, for it is God’s throne;
Oaths
35: or by the earth, for it is his footstool; or by Jerusalem, for it is the city of the Great King.
Oaths
36: And do not swear by your head, for you cannot make even one hair white or black.
Oaths
37: All you need to say is simply ‘Yes’ or ‘No’; anything beyond this comes from the evil one.
Eye for Eye
5:37 Or from evil
38: “You have heard that it was said, ‘Eye for eye, and tooth for tooth.’
Eye for Eye
39: But I tell you, do not resist an evil person. If anyone slaps you on the right cheek, turn to them the other cheek also.
Eye for Eye
40: And if anyone wants to sue you and take your shirt, hand over your coat as well.
Eye for Eye
41: If anyone forces you to go one mile, go with them two miles.
Eye for Eye
42: Give to the one who asks you, and do not turn away from the one who wants to borrow from you.
Love for Enemies
43: “You have heard that it was said, ‘Love your neighbor and hate your enemy.’
44: But I tell you, love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
Love for Enemies
45: that you may be children of your Father in heaven. He causes his sun to rise on the evil and the good, and sends rain on the righteous and the unrighteous.
46: If you love those who love you, what reward will you get? Are not even the tax collectors doing that?
47: And if you greet only your own people, what are you doing more than others? Do not even pagans do that?
48: Be perfect, therefore, as your heavenly Father is perfect.

1: “Be careful not to practice your righteousness in front of others to be seen by them. If you do, you will have no reward from your Father in heaven.
Giving to the Needy
2: “So when you give to the needy, do not announce it with trumpets, as the hypocrites do in the synagogues and on the streets, to be honored by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full.
Giving to the Needy
3: But when you give to the needy, do not let your left hand know what your right hand is doing,
Giving to the Needy
4: so that your giving may be in secret. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
Prayer
5: “And when you pray, do not be like the hypocrites, for they love to pray standing in the synagogues and on the street corners to be seen by others. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full.
Prayer
6: But when you pray, go into your room, close the door and pray to your Father, who is unseen. Then your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
Prayer
7: And when you pray, do not keep on babbling like pagans, for they think they will be heard because of their many words.
Prayer
8: Do not be like them, for your Father knows what you need before you ask him.
Prayer
9: “This, then, is how you should pray: “ ‘Our Father in heaven, hallowed be your name,
Prayer
10: your kingdom come, your will be done, on earth as it is in heaven.
Prayer
11: Give us today our daily bread.
Prayer
12: And forgive us our debts, as we also have forgiven our debtors.
Prayer
13: And lead us not into temptation, but deliver us from the evil one. ’
Prayer
for yours is the kingdom and the power and the glory forever. Amen.; 6:13  The Greek for  temptation  can also mean  testing./6:13  Or  from evil ; some late manuscripts  one,
14: For if you forgive other people when they sin against you, your heavenly Father will also forgive you.
Prayer
15: But if you do not forgive others their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins.
Fasting
16: “When you fast, do not look somber as the hypocrites do, for they disfigure their faces to show others they are fasting. Truly I tell you, they have received their reward in full.
Fasting
17: But when you fast, put oil on your head and wash your face,
Fasting
18: so that it will not be obvious to others that you are fasting, but only to your Father, who is unseen; and your Father, who sees what is done in secret, will reward you.
Treasures in Heaven
19: “Do not store up for yourselves treasures on earth, where moths and vermin destroy, and where thieves break in and steal.
Treasures in Heaven
20: But store up for yourselves treasures in heaven, where moths and vermin do not destroy, and where thieves do not break in and steal.
Treasures in Heaven
21: For where your treasure is, there your heart will be also.
Treasures in Heaven
22: “The eye is the lamp of the body. If your eyes are healthy, your whole body will be full of light.
Treasures in Heaven
6:22  The Greek for  healthy  here implies generous.
23: But if your eyes are unhealthy, your whole body will be full of darkness. If then the light within you is darkness, how great is that darkness!
Treasures in Heaven
6:23 The Greek for unhealthy here implies stingy.
24: “No one can serve two masters. Either you will hate the one and love the other, or you will be devoted to the one and despise the other. You cannot serve both God and money.
Do Not Worry
25: “Therefore I tell you, do not worry about your life, what you will eat or drink; or about your body, what you will wear. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothes?
Do Not Worry
26: Look at the birds of the air; they do not sow or reap or store away in barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not much more valuable than they?
Do Not Worry
27: Can any one of you by worrying add a single hour to your life ?
Do Not Worry
6:27 Or single cubit to your height
28: “And why do you worry about clothes? See how the flowers of the field grow. They do not labor or spin.
Do Not Worry
29: Yet I tell you that not even Solomon in all his splendor was dressed like one of these.
Do Not Worry
30: If that is how God clothes the grass of the field, which is here today and tomorrow is thrown into the fire, will he not much more clothe you—you of little faith?
Do Not Worry
31: So do not worry, saying, ‘What shall we eat?’ or ‘What shall we drink?’ or ‘What shall we wear?’
Do Not Worry
32: For the pagans run after all these things, and your heavenly Father knows that you need them.
Do Not Worry
33: But seek first his kingdom and his righteousness, and all these things will be given to you as well.
Do Not Worry
34: Therefore do not worry about tomorrow, for tomorrow will worry about itself. Each day has enough trouble of its own.
Do Not Worry

1: “Do not judge, or you too will be judged.
Judging Others
2: For in the same way you judge others, you will be judged, and with the measure you use, it will be measured to you.
Judging Others
3: “Why do you look at the speck of sawdust in your brother’s eye and pay no attention to the plank in your own eye?
Judging Others
4: How can you say to your brother, ‘Let me take the speck out of your eye,’ when all the time there is a plank in your own eye?
Judging Others
5: You hypocrite, first take the plank out of your own eye, and then you will see clearly to remove the speck from your brother’s eye.
Judging Others
6: “Do not give dogs what is sacred; do not throw your pearls to pigs. If you do, they may trample them under their feet, and turn and tear you to pieces.
Ask, Seek, Knock
7: “Ask and it will be given to you; seek and you will find; knock and the door will be opened to you.
Ask, Seek, Knock
8: For everyone who asks receives; the one who seeks finds; and to the one who knocks, the door will be opened.
Ask, Seek, Knock
9: “Which of you, if your son asks for bread, will give him a stone?
Ask, Seek, Knock
10: Or if he asks for a fish, will give him a snake?
Ask, Seek, Knock
11: If you, then, though you are evil, know how to give good gifts to your children, how much more will your Father in heaven give good gifts to those who ask him!
Ask, Seek, Knock
12: So in everything, do to others what you would have them do to you, for this sums up the Law and the Prophets.
The Narrow and Wide Gates
13: “Enter through the narrow gate. For wide is the gate and broad is the road that leads to destruction, and many enter through it.
The Narrow and Wide Gates
14: But small is the gate and narrow the road that leads to life, and only a few find it.
True and False Prophets
15: “Watch out for false prophets. They come to you in sheep’s clothing, but inwardly they are ferocious wolves.
True and False Prophets
16: By their fruit you will recognize them. Do people pick grapes from thornbushes, or figs from thistles?
True and False Prophets
17: Likewise, every good tree bears good fruit, but a bad tree bears bad fruit.
True and False Prophets
18: A good tree cannot bear bad fruit, and a bad tree cannot bear good fruit.
True and False Prophets
19: Every tree that does not bear good fruit is cut down and thrown into the fire.
True and False Prophets
20: Thus, by their fruit you will recognize them.
True and False Disciples
21: “Not everyone who says to me, ‘Lord, Lord,’ will enter the kingdom of heaven, but only the one who does the will of my Father who is in heaven.
True and False Disciples
22: Many will say to me on that day, ‘Lord, Lord, did we not prophesy in your name and in your name drive out demons and in your name perform many miracles?’
True and False Disciples
23: Then I will tell them plainly, ‘I never knew you. Away from me, you evildoers!’
The Wise and Foolish Builders
24: “Therefore everyone who hears these words of mine and puts them into practice is like a wise man who built his house on the rock.
The Wise and Foolish Builders
25: The rain came down, the streams rose, and the winds blew and beat against that house; yet it did not fall, because it had its foundation on the rock.
The Wise and Foolish Builders
26: But everyone who hears these words of mine and does not put them into practice is like a foolish man who built his house on sand.
The Wise and Foolish Builders
